defs: An American animal allied to the weasel.

records.ety: [Massachusetts Indian asq, pl. asquash, raw, green, immature, applied to fruit and vegetables which were used when green, or without cooking; askutasquash vine apple.]

defs: A plant and its fruit of the genus Cucurbita, or gourd kind.
notes: ☞ The species are much confused. The long-neck squash is called Cucurbita verrucosa, the Barbary or China squash, Cucurbita moschata, and the great winter squash, Cucurbita maxima, but the distinctions are not clear.

records.ety: [OE. squachen, OF. escachier, esquachier, to squash, to crush, F. cacher, perhaps from (assumed) LL. excoacticare, fr. L. ex + coactare to constrain, from cogere, coactum, to compel. Cf. Cogent, Squat, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Squashed (skwsht); p. pr. & vb. n. Squashing.]

defs: To beat or press into pulp or a flat mass; to crush.
Something soft and easily crushed; especially, an unripe pod of pease.
quotes: Not yet old enough for a man, nor young enough for a boy; as a squash is before 't is a peascod.

defs: Hence, something unripe or soft; -- used in contempt.
A sudden fall of a heavy, soft body; also, a shock of soft bodies.
quotes: My fall was stopped by a terrible squash.

defs: A game much like rackets, played in a walled court with soft rubber balls and bats like tennis rackets; -- called also squash rackets.
